rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical 1,2-bromo-alkoxylation of alkenes using CH2Br2 as bromine source and alcohol as alkoxy source/solvent in an undivided cell to afford β-bromoethers
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of Br- (released from CH2Br2 C-X cleavage) to Br• radical, which adds to the alkene; the resulting C-radical is further oxidized at the anode to a benzylic carbocation; at the cathode, protons are reduced to H• (H2 evolution) and +CH2Br is reduced to give CH3Br

key_intermediates.name: +CH2Br bromomethyl cation from CH2Br2 C-X cleavage
benzylic C-centered radical from Br• addition to alkene (II)
benzylic carbocation from anodic oxidation of II (III)
alkyl oxonium salt from ROH attack on carbocation (IV)
hydrogen radical H• from cathodic reduction of H+
CH3Br byproduct from H•/+CH2Br combination at cathode

electron_flow: C-X bond cleavage of CH2Br2 releases Br- and +CH2Br; anodic oxidation of Br- gives Br• which adds to the alkene to form a benzylic radical; a second anodic oxidation converts this radical to a benzylic carbocation that is trapped by ROH to give an oxonium, which deprotonates to the β-bromoether. At the cathode, H+ is reduced to H• (giving H2) and +CH2Br is reduced to CH3Br.

probe_reagents.name: BHT (2,6-di-tert-butyl-4-methylphenol)
probe_reagents.observation: Yield of 1a drastically diminished to 45%; BHT-Br adduct detected by GC-MS (m/z = 299), supporting a free-radical (Br•) intermediate

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethylene (substrate)
probe_reagents.observation: CV oxidation peak at 2.48 V vs Ag/AgCl, higher than CH2Br2 (1.93 V), indicating CH2Br2/Br- is oxidized preferentially

probe_reagents.name: CH2Br2
probe_reagents.observation: CV oxidation peak at 1.93 V vs Ag/AgCl, assigned to Br- → Br• oxidation

probe_reagents.name: no-current control
probe_reagents.observation: No product formation without applied current, confirming electrochemical nature

notes: Cathodic chemistry (H• generation and +CH2Br reduction to CH3Br) is proposed in parallel with the anodic catalytic cycle, making this effectively a paired electrolysis. iPrOH and tBuOH failed as coupling partners, suggesting steric limits on the oxonium/nucleophilic addition step. No KIE or DFT studies were performed.
